step_by_step_thinking:
In disasters, conditions like poor sanitation can exacerbate diarrheal diseases leading to transmission. Similarly, acute respiratory infections can be easily transmitted through air or droplets due to crowding. However, measles is typically not as widespread post-disaster because it requires close contact for prolonged periods which may decrease given chaotic environments. Malnutrition does not directly cause communicable disease but rather impairs immune function making individuals more susceptible.
